results.title: Sand mandala — Wikipedia
results.judge_reason: This is the canonical source-domain reference exactly describing the sand-mandala construction-dissolution ritual with vertical/horizontal cuts and impermanence teaching.

results.title: Why Monks Destroy Mandalas
results.judge_reason: Direct overlap with source-domain content_words covering impermanence, construction, and ritual destruction of sand mandalas.

results.title: Tibetan Sand Mandalas — World History Encyclopedia
results.judge_reason: Covers the same source-domain construction+destruction process matching multiple source-side content words.

results.title: ZK-APEX: Zero-Knowledge Approximate Personalized Unlearning with Executable Proofs (2025/12)
results.judge_reason: ZK-SNARK verifiable unlearning proof is essentially the candidate's 'destruction certificate' mechanism applied to LLM weights/data.

results.title: Reasoning Model Unlearning: Forgetting Traces, Not Just Answers (2025/6)
results.judge_reason: Explicitly targets forgetting reasoning traces while keeping capability — the exact LLM-side framing of the candidate proposal.

results.title: Secure Forgetting: Privacy-Driven Unlearning for LLM Agents (2026/4)
results.judge_reason: Privacy-driven trajectory/state unlearning for agents overlaps strongly with the candidate's privacy-preserving reasoning-trace destruction objective.

results.title: Unlearning Isn't Invisible (2025/6)
results.judge_reason: Discusses formal certification of unlearning and detectable fingerprints — directly engages the candidate's verifiable-destruction-certificate claim.

results.title: Agents Are All You Need for LLM Unlearning (2025/2)
results.judge_reason: Agent-based inference-time unlearning addresses the same goal — make reasoning-derived knowledge non-recoverable from the deployed agent.
